title: Mindset
subtitle: The New Psychology of Success
author: Carol S. Dweck
themes: There are two mindsets: fixed (belief that abilities are static) and growth (belief that abilities can be developed)
The mindset you hold shapes your behavior, relationships, and life outcomes
Praise for intelligence creates a fixed mindset; praise for effort creates a growth mindset
Growth mindset leads to resilience — viewing challenges as opportunities to learn
Fixed mindset leads to avoidance of challenges and defensiveness in the face of setbacks
Mindset can be changed — awareness is the first step
excerpt: For twenty years, my research has shown that the view you adopt for yourself profoundly affects the way you lead your life. It can determine whether you become the person you want to be and whether you accomplish the things you value. How does this happen? How can a simple belief have the power to transform your psychology and, as a result, your life?
